flashcards.front: What are the key fields in an IPv4 header?
flashcards.back: An IPv4 header contains: Version (4 bits), IHL (4 bits), Type of Service (8 bits), Total Length (16 bits), Identification (16 bits), Flags (3 bits), Fragment Offset (13 bits), TTL (8 bits), Protocol (8 bits), Header Checksum (16 bits), Source IP (32 bits), Destination IP (32 bits), and Options (variable).

flashcards.front: Describe the IPv6 header structure and its new features.
flashcards.back: IPv6 header has 8 fixed fields: Version (4 bits), Traffic Class (8 bits), Flow Label (20 bits), Payload Length (16 bits), Next Header (8 bits), Hop Limit (8 bits), Source Address (128 bits), Destination Address (128 bits). New features include: Flow Label for QoS, larger address space (128-bit), no checksum (improved router performance), and extension headers for optional functions.

flashcards.front: Compare IPv4 and IPv6 addressing schemes.
flashcards.back: IPv4 uses 32-bit addresses (~4.3 billion addresses) with dotted decimal notation (e.g., 192.168.1.1). IPv6 uses 128-bit addresses (~3.4×10³⁸ addresses) with hexadecimal notation grouped in 8 groups of 4 hex digits (e.g., 2001:0db8:85a3:0000:0000:8a2e:0370:7334). IPv6 eliminates NAT and provides auto-configuration.

flashcards.front: What is CIDR notation? Given IP 192.168.10.0/24, how many subnets and hosts per subnet are possible if we borrow 4 bits for subnetting?
flashcards.back: CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) uses IP/mask format to represent networks. Borrowing 4 bits from host portion: Subnet mask = 255.255.255.240 (/28). Number of subnets = 2⁴ = 16 subnets. Hosts per subnet = 2⁴ - 2 = 14 usable hosts (减去网络地址和广播地址).

flashcards.front: What are the IPv6 address types?
flashcards.back: IPv6 has three main address types: Unicast (single interface - Global Unicast, Link-Local, Unique Local), Multicast (group of interfaces - replaces IPv4 broadcast), and Anycast (nearest member of group). IPv6 has no broadcast address; multicast is used instead for all broadcast functions.

flashcards.front: Calculate the network address and broadcast address for IP 172.16.45.130 with subnet mask 255.255.255.192.
What are the advantages of IPv6 over IPv4 for Delhi University network infrastructure?
flashcards.back: IPv6 advantages: (1) Vast address space eliminates address exhaustion, (2) Simplified header improves router efficiency, (3) Built-in security with IPsec, (4) Stateless Address Autoconfiguration (SLAAC), (5) Better QoS with Flow Label, (6) No need for NAT, enabling true end-to-end connectivity, (7) Simplified network management and routing tables.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between Classful and Classless addressing? How does VLSM help in efficient IP allocation?
flashcards.back: Classful addressing (pre-1993) divides IP into fixed classes: A(/8), B(/16), C(/24) with wasted addresses. Classless (CIDR) uses variable-length subnet masks (VLSM) allowing custom subnet sizes. VLSM enables subnetting subnets, optimizing address utilization by allocating smaller subnets to point-to-point links (/30 or /31) and larger subnets to LANs, crucial for large campus networks like DU.
